articles.title: The Moment That Broke Conor McGregor #ufc
articles.description: One question from Max Holloway appeared to completely break Conor McGregor emotionally. After the fight, Max asked Conor, "Why did you come back?" According to Chael Sonnen, McGregor immediately covered his face, turned away, and collapsed to the canvas. It was one of the most emotional moments of Conor's career. But Chael says fans misunderstood the exchange. After speaking with Holloway's team, Chael learned that the question wasn't intended as an insult. Max wasn't telling McGregor he didn't belong or that his fighting days were over. In fact, it was supposed to be the beginning of a conversation. The idea, according to Chael, was that Conor would answer, "Because I'm a warrior," and Max would respond with words of encouragement and respect. The conversation simply never got that far. Chael gives Holloway a tremendous amount of credit. He moved up in weight, took a major risk, and delivered one of the biggest wins of his career. But he also had opportunities to take shots at McGregor throughout fight week and never did. That's why Chael believes the story. He doesn't think Max was trying to humiliate Conor. He believes Holloway was trying to offer a quick message of respect to a fellow warrior who was clearly hurting. For more UFC analysis, Conor McGregor updates, Max Holloway news, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #ChaelSonnen #MMA #CombatSports #UFCReturn #FightNews

articles.title: The Heavyweights Are Scared of Gable #ufc
articles.description: According to Chael Sonnen, the UFC heavyweight division should be lining up to fight Gable Steveson—but they won't. In this breakdown, Chael praises Steveson's performance and believes many fans are overlooking just how impressive it was. Gable showed composure, slipped punches, managed distance, and pressured forward without looking like a wrestler desperately searching for takedowns. In fact, he never landed a takedown all night. Instead, Chael points out that Steveson used the threat of wrestling exactly the way elite fighters should—forcing opponents to react and creating openings for his striking. His knees, kicks, and overall striking game impressed Chael, as did his ability to listen to his corner and handle the pressure of the moment. So what's next? Chael says every heavyweight should be calling Gable out immediately because fighting a rising star is how careers are made. But he doesn't expect that to happen. His reason is simple: he believes many fighters talk about wanting the toughest challenges, but when a dangerous new contender like Steveson arrives, they're suddenly not so eager. For Chael, Gable's performance proved one thing above all else: The heavyweight division has a serious new player—and some people already seem nervous about it. For more UFC analysis, Gable Steveson updates, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #GableSteveson #ChaelSonnen #Heavyweight #MMA #Wrestling #CombatSports #UFCFightNight

articles.title: Attention, Gable Critics...
articles.description: Gable Steveson's latest performance has drawn criticism from some who believe he still lacks experience. Chael Sonnen sees it very differently. Chael explains why Gable wasn't just another wrestling prospect, but one of the most accomplished athletes to ever transition into MMA. From his dominance before college to becoming the youngest American Olympic wrestling champion in history, Chael argues that many critics simply don't know Gable's background. While some focused on what they viewed as flaws, Chael saw composure, elite athleticism, and moments that suggest Gable's ceiling is much higher than people realize. He also asks the question nobody seems willing to answer: if Gable isn't ready, who should he fight next? articles.title: Robert Whittaker’s NOT Fat, He’s a Contender
articles.description: Robert Whittaker's move to light heavyweight sparked plenty of discussion about his appearance, but Chael Sonnen believes people are missing the bigger picture. Chael explains why adding quality muscle takes years—not weeks—and points to Jon Jones as the perfect example. Jones spent three years adding just a handful of pounds before moving to heavyweight, while Whittaker jumped to 205 without taking any time off because he simply wanted to keep competing. More importantly, Chael argues that former champions who successfully move up in weight have historically been rewarded with an immediate path to another title. Instead of criticizing Whittaker's physique, the conversation should be about where he belongs in the light heavyweight title picture. articles.title: Max Holloway Picked the Wrong Fight #ufc
articles.description: Chael Sonnen believes Max Holloway made the biggest mistake of the night—and it had nothing to do with what happened inside the cage. After defeating Conor McGregor, Holloway called for a trilogy fight, saying he doesn't care how long he has to wait to run it back. Chael strongly disagrees with that approach. In his view, when a fighter is injured and clearly dealing with physical setbacks, the proper response is grace, not immediately asking for another fight. Chael argues that Holloway had several intriguing options available and didn't need to target an injured McGregor. Instead, he points to massive opportunities elsewhere. A showdown with Arman Tsarukyan would have major implications in the lightweight division. A fight with Paddy Pimblett could be one of the biggest attractions in the UFC. For Chael, the issue isn't whether a McGregor trilogy could eventually happen. It's the timing of the callout. He believes Holloway defeated a version of Conor that never should have been in that position physically, making the immediate challenge for a third fight feel unnecessary. Love it or hate it, Chael says the biggest miss of the evening wasn't a strike inside the Octagon—it was Max's decision to call for another fight with McGregor. For more UFC analysis, Conor McGregor updates, Max Holloway news, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #MaxHolloway #ConorMcGregor #ChaelSonnen #PaddyPimblett #ArmanTsarukyan #MMA #CombatSports

articles.title: 🚨 DANA WHITE EPIC RANT: “WE ARE THE WORST EVER!” 😭🔥
articles.description: Dana White delivered a hilarious, brutally honest rant after the UFC production team reportedly identified boxing star Shakur Stevenson as an Oklahoma City NBA player during the broadcast. 😳 Dana praised the rest of the production as nearly flawless—but admitted the UFC somehow continues to struggle whenever celebrities appear on screen: “We put up graphics and put the wrong guy’s name on it. We are the worst ever to do it.” After 25 years, Dana joked that the UFC may need to stop showing celebrities altogether. articles.title: The UFC’s Next Massive Star? Paddy Pimblett #ufc
articles.description: According to Chael Sonnen, Paddy Pimblett isn't just popular—he's a genuine superstar. In this breakdown, Chael explains why Paddy "The Baddy" has become one of the biggest attractions in the UFC. The energy inside the arena during his walkout was unlike almost anything Chael has ever seen. Fans were on their feet, celebrities were reacting, and the atmosphere felt bigger than a typical co-main event. Chael even says Paddy is the kind of fighter who inspires the next generation. He's the guy kids watch and say, "I want to do that. I want to be like him." As for what's next, Chael believes there are massive fights waiting for Pimblett. Potential matchups with Arman Tsarukyan, Ilia Topuria, and Justin Gaethje could all shape the future of the lightweight division. He also mentions a fight with Max Holloway, though he isn't convinced the UFC will make that one. One prediction Chael is willing to make? Paddy's days as a co-main event fighter are over. The UFC has a star on its hands, and the crowd's reaction proved it. For more UFC analysis, Paddy Pimblett updates, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #PaddyPimblett #PaddyTheBaddy #ChaelSonnen #MMA #IliaTopuria #JustinGaethje #ArmanTsarukyan

articles.title: ‘Conor Owes Him!’ Izzy & DJ On Conor vs Chandler
Conor vs. Floyd: Broken vs. BROKE
articles.description: Do your hands belong on the permanent record? Register them today at https://registeryourhands.com Conor McGregor's comeback didn't end the way anyone hoped, but Chael Sonnen doesn't believe it should be the final chapter. After returning from a devastating leg injury, a six-year layoff, and competing above his ideal weight class, Chael explains why Conor was facing nearly impossible odds from the start. Instead of rushing back into another MMA fight, he explores whether boxing could be the smarter path forward. Could a rematch with Floyd Mayweather actually make sense? Chael breaks down why boxing eliminates one of Conor's biggest obstacles, what conditions would need to be met for the fight to happen, and why "Broken vs. Broke" might be the most interesting idea on the table. articles.title: 🚨 DANA WHITE: CONOR McGREGOR IS THE BIGGEST SUPERSTAR IN ANY SPORT! 😳🔥
articles.description: Dana White gave credit to every generation of UFC stars for helping grow the sport—from Chuck Liddell and Matt Hughes to Anderson Silva, Georges St-Pierre, Ronda Rousey, and Conor McGregor. But Dana made one thing clear: “Conor is obviously the biggest superstar, not just in this sport, but in any sport.” McGregor may not have built the UFC alone, but Dana believes no fighter has reached a bigger level of worldwide fame. Is Conor McGregor the biggest combat-sports star of all time? 👇🔥 Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/

articles.title: What Really Happened to Conor’s Leg? #ufc
articles.description: Did Conor McGregor suffer another serious injury, and what does it mean for his future? In this breakdown, Chael Sonnen revisits a key moment from McGregor's return and explains why he saw something very different than many viewers. While much of the conversation centered around Conor's kicks, Chael noticed that McGregor appeared to lose his balance while throwing a punch. To him, it looked less like a specific technique failing and more like an elite athlete who simply hasn't competed in six years. Chael also addresses comments made by Joe Rogan, who suggested on the broadcast that McGregor may have suffered a significant knee injury. That could prove to be accurate, but Chael says he didn't see enough to confidently make that conclusion. The bigger question, as always, is: what's next? If there is a serious knee injury involved, the timeline becomes difficult. Recovery could take a year before McGregor even returns to full training, and the possibility of another long layoff raises obvious questions about whether another comeback is realistic. At some point, every sports legend faces the same crossroads. Do we wait for one more run? Or do we simply thank Conor McGregor for the memories and appreciate one of the most unforgettable careers in UFC history? For more UFC analysis, Conor McGregor updates, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #ConorMcGregor #ChaelSonnen #JoeRogan #MMA #UFCReturn #CombatSports #FightNews

articles.title: Has Conor Reached the End? #ufc
articles.description: What happens next for Conor McGregor if he wants one more fight? In this breakdown, Chael Sonnen explains why a return is still possible—but under very different circumstances. If McGregor comes back and says he simply wants to compete again, Chael believes fans and the UFC would support it. But it would have to be driven by one thing: the love of fighting. The bigger question is whether the UFC can sell the same formula twice. Can you once again put McGregor in a weight class that isn't his own, match him against another star from a different division, attach no title implications, and still make it the main event? Chael doesn't think so. He argues that if McGregor fights again, the terms may have to change. It might not be a five-round fight, and for the first time in his UFC career, it might not close the show. That doesn't diminish what McGregor has accomplished. Chael credits Conor for representing the sport at the highest level for a very long time. But as a former fighter himself, Chael understands a difficult truth about combat sports. A fighter is never done with the business. Eventually, though, the business becomes done with the fighter. For more UFC analysis, Conor McGregor updates, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #ConorMcGregor #ChaelSonnen #MMA #CombatSports #UFCReturn #FightNews

Jon Jones has built his legacy inside the Octagon, but Chael Sonnen believes some of the best examples of Jon's greatness have happened away from official competition. Chael shares several stories of Jon casually accepting grappling challenges at public events, explains why these moments reveal just how naturally gifted he is, and compares them to the legendary stories that helped build Bruce Lee's mystique. From playful sparring sessions to effortless takedowns, Chael argues these unscripted moments say as much about Jon Jones' greatness as any championship fight. articles.title: Gable Steveson Just Changed the Heavyweight Division
articles.description: Gable Steveson impressed in his latest performance, but Chael Sonnen believes the biggest story isn't how well he fought—it's how the rest of the heavyweight division is reacting. Chael breaks down Gable's dramatic weight loss, why his striking looked far more polished than many expected, and why simply threatening the takedown made his stand-up game so effective. More importantly, Chael explains why every ranked heavyweight should be calling Gable out immediately—and why the silence says everything. According to Chael, fighting Gable Steveson isn't just about competition. It's the fastest way to get featured, build a profile, and advance a heavyweight career. articles.title: The Truth About Bobby Green vs. Terrance McKinney
articles.description: Bobby Green and Terrance McKinney delivered one of the wildest fights of UFC 223, but the conversation quickly shifted from the action inside the cage to rumors online. Chael Sonnen explains why the stoppage was the correct call, why referee Kerry Hatley handled the situation exactly as he should have, and why Bobby Green and Terrance McKinney's prior relationship shouldn't fuel conspiracy theories. Chael also addresses the online speculation surrounding the fight, explains what officials are looking for when a fighter turtles up under pressure, and shares why he believes this was a clean, honest fight from start to finish. articles.title: Max Holloway Blew His Biggest Moment
articles.description: Thanks to Bear Mattress for sponsoring this video! Visit https://bearmattress.com/chael and use CODE: chael to get 40% off your Bear mattress! Max Holloway picked up one of the biggest wins of his career, but Chael Sonnen believes what happened after the fight may have overshadowed the victory. Instead of focusing on the performance, Max used his post-fight press conference to call for a trilogy with Conor McGregor. Chael explains why that was the wrong move, why fighters should show grace after an opponent is injured, and why Max missed a much bigger opportunity to call out the names that could have defined the next chapter of his career. Chael also compares the situation to Vitor Belfort vs. Evander Holyfield and explains how public perception can completely change the way a victory is remembered. articles.title: 🚨 PADDY PIMBLETT: “HE WAS LIKE A DEAD BODY ON THE FLOOR” 😳🔥
articles.description: 🚨 PADDY PIMBLETT: “HE WAS LIKE A DEAD BODY ON THE FLOOR” 😳🔥 Paddy Pimblett says he predicted exactly how his UFC 329 fight with Benoit Saint Denis would end. “The Baddy” expected Saint Denis to shoot for an early takedown—and believed the moment he did, his neck would be exposed. “I knew I was going to choke him.” Pimblett submitted Saint Denis in just 52 seconds and said he had to alert referee Marc Goddard when his opponent went limp: “He was like a dead body on the floor… I said to Goddard, ‘He’s out.’” Was this Paddy Pimblett’s most dominant UFC performance yet? 👇🔥 Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/

articles.description: Dana White addressed the latest talk of peace with Eddie Hearn following comments involving Turki Al-Sheikh. articles.title: 🚨 DANA WHITE: McGREGOR RETIREMENT or HOLLOWAY REMATCH 👀
articles.description: DANA WHITE: McGREGOR RETIREMENT or MAX HOLLOWAY 3 REMATCH Dana White was asked whether Conor McGregor should retire following the shocking ending to UFC 329. Dana clarified that he believes fighters should walk away when they begin seriously talking or thinking about retirement—but said he has not heard that from McGregor yet. When asked whether the UFC would book an immediate rematch with Max Holloway if Conor requests it, White would not commit: “There are a lot of unanswered questions right now.” Dana says the UFC will wait to hear from McGregor, assess the injury and discuss the future once everyone returns to the office. REMATCH or RETIRE? 👇 Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/

articles.title: 🚨 PADDY PIMBLETT: “I’M GOING TO BE WORLD CHAMPION—ANYONE CAN GET IT!” 🔥
articles.description: After submitting Benoit Saint Denis at UFC 329, Paddy Pimblett made his championship ambitions crystal clear. When asked who he wants next, “The Baddy” said the opponent does not matter: “I want to be the first-ever world champion from Liverpool, and I’m going to do that.” Whether it takes one more fight or ten, Paddy believes becoming undisputed UFC champion is his destiny—and he says anyone can get it, including Justin Gaethje or Charles Oliveira. Who should Paddy Pimblett fight next? 👇🔥 Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/

articles.title: 🚨 WHAT MOTIVATES PADDY PIMBLETT?!!! 🔥
articles.description: Paddy Pimblett revealed the real motivation behind his spectacular UFC 329 submission victory over Benoit Saint Denis. A reporter asked whether England’s World Cup win fired him up before the fight, but Paddy said his motivation comes from somewhere much closer to home: “I have little twin babies at home, and they give me more motivation than anything in this world.” Pimblett said everything he does is about providing for his children, putting food in their mouths, clothes on their backs, and continuing to win. Family first. Fighter second. ❤️👊 Does becoming a father make Paddy even more dangerous? 👇 Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/

articles.title: 🚨 PADDY PIMBLETT: “MY JIU-JITSU IS THE BEST IN THE WORLD!” 🔥
articles.description: 🚨 PADDY PIMBLETT INVENTED THE FINISH ON THE FLY! “MY JIU-JITSU IS THE BEST IN THE WORLD!” 🔥 Paddy Pimblett revealed that his spectacular UFC 329 submission of Benoit Saint Denis was created in real time. Paddy said the finish was a hybrid of techniques he had drilled in camp, including elements of a Peruvian necktie, but admitted he had never completed that exact submission before: “I just did that on the fly. That’s how I roll.” “The Baddy” believes his ability to connect techniques seamlessly proves his jiu-jitsu belongs among the very best in MMA.
